meta.title: IELTS Speaking Question Bank — Research Report

questions: Would you like to play in a public garden or park?
What do you like to do when visiting a park?
How are the parks today different from those you visited as a kid?
Would you prefer to play in a personal garden or public garden?
part1.source: 紫藤雅思2025年5-8月口语题库解析

questions: Have you ever learnt about outer space and stars?
Are you interested in films about outer space and stars?
Do you want to go to outer space in the future?
Do you want to know more about outer space?
part1.source: 2024年第三季度雅思口语机经库

questions: Do you like science?
When did you start to learn about science?
What is your favourite subject of science?
What kinds of interesting things have you done with science?
Do you like watching science TV programs?
Do Chinese people often visit science museums?
part1.source: 2024年第三季度雅思口语机经库

questions: Do you think the colour of a car is important to you?
What kinds of car would you most like to have in the future?
Did you enjoy travelling by car when you were young?
Would you prefer to be a driver or a passenger in a car?
part1.source: 2024年9-12月雅思口语题库

questions: When did you start using social media?
Do you think you spend too much time on social media?
Do your friends use social media?
What do people often do on social media?
part1.source: 2024年5-8月最新雅思口语题库

questions: Did you wear a watch when you were young?
Have you ever received a watch as a gift?
Do you usually wear a watch now?
Why do you think some people like to wear expensive watches?
part1.source: 2024年9-12月雅思口语题库

questions: What kinds of websites do you use most often?
Do you prefer to get information from websites or books?
Which is more important to you, what a website looks like or how easy it is to use?
Does your favourite website look the same now as when you first used it?
part1.source: 2024年9-12月雅思口语题库

questions: How often do you use a mirror?
Would you buy clothes without checking yourself in a mirror?
Have you ever bought a mirror?
Do you think mirrors are a good way to decorate a room?
part1.source: 2024年9-12月雅思口语题库

questions: Have you ever sent handmade gifts to others?
Have you ever received a great gift?
What do you consider when choosing a gift?
Do you think you are good at choosing gifts?
What gift have you received recently?
part1.source: 紫藤雅思2026年1-4月口语题库

questions: What made you happy when you were little?
Is there anything that makes you feel happy lately?
What do you think will make you feel happy in the future?
Are you happy when you buy new things?
When do you feel happy at work? Why?
Do you think happiness is important?
Do you think people are happy when buying new things?
part1.source: 2024年第三季度雅思口语机经库 (Happiness topic)

questions: Where did you go in your last holiday?
Which public holiday do you like the best?
What do you usually do in your holidays?
Do you like holidays? Why?
Do you like to spend your day at home?
part1.source: 2024年第三季度雅思口语机经库

questions: Do you often go to the library?
What do you usually do in the library?
Did you go to the library when you were a kid?
Do children in your country often go to the library?
part1.source: 2024年第三季度雅思口语机经库

questions: Do you use headphones often?
What kind of headphones do you prefer?
When do you usually wear headphones?
Do you think it's rude to wear headphones when talking to others?
part1.source: Synthesized from IELTS web sources

questions: Do you enjoy watching interesting and funny movies?
Do you think you are a funny person?
Are you a person who often makes others laugh?
Do you always laugh at funny things?
When was the last time you laughed?
part1.source: 雅思口语2025年1-4月最新 (Laughing topic)

questions: What kind of clothes do you like to wear?
Do you prefer to wear comfortable and casual clothes or smart clothes?
Do you like wearing T-shirts?
Do you spend a lot of time choosing clothes?
part1.source: 2024年5-8月最新雅思口语题库

questions: Do you like singing? Why?
Have you ever learned how to sing?
Who do you want to sing for?
Do you think singing can bring happiness to people?
part1.source: 2024年5-8月最新雅思口语题库

questions: Have you ever used any AI tools or applications?
How do you think AI will change our daily lives?
Do you think AI is more helpful or more dangerous?
Would you trust an AI to make important decisions for you?
What kind of jobs do you think AI will replace in the future?
part1.source: Synthesized from IELTS web sources (2026 new topic)

questions: Are you concerned about your privacy online?
What kind of personal information do you share on the internet?
Do you think companies should be allowed to collect users' data?
Have you ever had an experience where your privacy was violated?
What can people do to protect their digital privacy?
part1.source: Synthesized from IELTS web sources (2026 new topic)

questions: Have you ever worked or studied from home?
What are the advantages of working remotely?
Do you think remote work is more productive than working in an office?
What kind of jobs can be done remotely?
Do you think remote work will become more common in the future?
part1.source: Synthesized from IELTS web sources (2026 new topic)

questions: How often do you communicate with others online?
Do you prefer online communication or face-to-face communication?
What apps do you use most for online communication?
Do you think online communication can replace in-person meetings?
Has online communication affected your relationships with family and friends?
part1.source: Synthesized from IELTS web sources (2026 new topic)

part2.topic: Famous Person You Would Like to Meet

cueCard.explain: And explain why you would like to meet him/her

keyInsights.title: ~60% carry-over rate
keyInsights.detail: About 60% of May-Aug 2026 Part 2 topics are recycled from previous seasons. Only ~37 out of 134 compiled topics appear truly new.

keyInsights.title: Most persistent topics
keyInsights.detail: "Movie You Watched/Enjoyed" and "Sport/Activity You Enjoy" appear in ALL 7 seasons — the most evergreen IELTS cue cards.

keyInsights.title: AI & Digital trend
keyInsights.detail: 2026 introduces AI ("Time You Used AI to Solve a Problem", "Person Who Is a Great Digital Communicator") and digital privacy as new themes.

keyInsights.title: Part 1 most stable
keyInsights.detail: "Art", "Science", "Parks", and "Gifts" appear in every single season (7/7). Safe bets for preparation.

recommendations: Prioritize the Top 20 Part 2 topics — they've appeared in 4–7 out of 7 seasons.
Balance preparation between evergreen topics and new 2026 additions (~28% new).
Build AI & tech vocabulary — new cue cards require domain terminology.
Master the 7 all-season Part 1 topics first — highest predictability.
